settings-singleton-with-csv-fallback.name: settings-singleton-with-csv-fallback
settings-singleton-with-csv-fallback.description: Pydantic Settings singleton that centralizes all environment/.env configuration, with a custom source mixin allowing comma-separated strings for List fields despite pydantic-settings' JSON-first parsing.
invariants: MANAGED_DOMAINS must parse from either JSON list or comma-separated string
KEY_TYPE is always normalized to lowercase 'rsa' or 'ecc'
DOMAIN_KEY_SIZE >= 2048 when KEY_TYPE='rsa'
ACME_EAB_KEY_ID and ACME_EAB_HMAC_KEY must be set together, never partially, for digicert/zerossl/sectigo
WEBROOT_PATH required when HTTP_CHALLENGE_MODE='webroot'
CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN/GOOGLE_PROJECT_ID required for their respective DNS_PROVIDER when HTTP_CHALLENGE_MODE='dns'
ACME_DIRECTORY_URL is resolved from CA_PROVIDER presets unless CA_PROVIDER='custom'
contracts: settings is a module-level singleton importable everywhere
Settings() raises ValueError at construction time for any invalid combination, never silently degrades
LLM_DISABLED=False requires langchain to be importable or construction fails

cli-command-registry-dispatch.name: cli-command-registry-dispatch
cli-command-registry-dispatch.description: argparse-based CLI that maps mutually exclusive action flags to handler closures via an ordered command_registry dict, executing only the first matching command.
invariants: At least one primary action flag must be selected or help is printed and process exits 1
--expiring-in-30-days and --domain-status are strictly read-only and never mutate certificate state
--generate-test-cert never contacts ACME servers
contracts: run_once/run_revocation accept an optional settings override for dependency injection in tests/MCP
CLI exits with sys.exit(1) and logs an error before doing so on invalid configuration

runtime-settings-override-via-singleton-.name: runtime-settings-override-via-singleton-replacement
runtime-settings-override-via-singleton-.description: Mechanism for one-shot CLI/MCP overrides (CA provider, ACME directory URL) by rebuilding a new Settings object from model_dump() and reassigning config.settings, rather than mutating fields in place.
invariants: build_settings_from_override returns the original settings object unchanged if no overrides given
Overriding always re-validates via Settings(**data), so invalid overrides still raise
contracts: apply_runtime_settings_overrides mutates the global config.settings singleton as a side effect
Callers wanting isolation must use _temporary_settings_override context manager (see mcp_server.py) rather than this function directly

mcp-server-tool-surface.name: mcp-server-tool-surface
mcp-server-tool-surface.description: FastMCP server exposing renewal/revocation/status operations as async tools, using an explicit ca_input_mode ('config'|'custom') to disambiguate whether tool calls should read global config or use caller-supplied CA overrides.
invariants: State-mutating tools (renew_once, revoke_cert, generate_test_cert, health) must acquire _MCP_OPERATION_LOCK before running
Read-only tools (expiring_in_30_days, expiring_within, list_managed_domains, read_cert_details, domain_status) never acquire the lock (required=False)
ca_input_mode='custom' requires both ca_provider and acme_directory_url; acme_directory_url must start with http:// or https://
_temporary_settings_override always restores original config.settings in a finally block, even on exception
generate_test_cert domain input is validated against path traversal before being joined onto CERT_STORE_PATH
contracts: Every tool returns a dict with at least a 'status' key ('success'|'failed') and never raises to the MCP transport
All exceptions are caught, logged via logger.exception, and converted into {'status': 'failed', 'error': str(e)}

jsonl-structured-logging-with-run-id.name: jsonl-structured-logging-with-run-id
jsonl-structured-logging-with-run-id.description: Singleton logger wrapping stdlib logging with a JSONLFormatter (one JSON object per line) and a RunIDFilter injecting a per-process UUID into every record, implemented via a decorator pattern over logging.Logger.
invariants: Every log line includes timestamp, level, run_id, logger, message as top-level JSON keys
Exception tracebacks are JSON-encoded as a single 'exc_info' string field, never raw newlines, to keep output valid JSONL
run_id is stable for the lifetime of the process (singleton, set once in __init__)
contracts: logger.py exports a single 'logger' instance; all modules should import and use this rather than instantiating their own
Extra fields passed via logging's extra= kwarg are merged verbatim into the JSON payload

renewal-graph-topology.name: renewal-graph-topology
renewal-graph-topology.description: LangGraph StateGraph defining the certificate renewal lifecycle: scan → plan → per-domain loop (order → challenge → CSR → finalize → download → store) with conditional routing for no-renewals-needed, challenge failure, and end-of-loop.
invariants: renewal_planner always precedes acme_account_setup; if no domains need renewal, the graph short-circuits directly to summary_reporter
error_handler retries always route through retry_scheduler (never directly back to pick_next_domain) so backoff is always applied before a retry
pick_next_domain is the sole loop entry point and must reset current_order/current_nonce/retry_count when starting a new domain
contracts: build_graph returns a compiled graph ready for .invoke()/.stream()
initial_state() is the only sanctioned way to construct a valid starting AgentState dict for this graph

revocation-graph-topology.name: revocation-graph-topology
revocation-graph-topology.description: Separate, simpler StateGraph for certificate revocation reusing acme_account_setup but with its own domain-loop nodes; deliberately has no error_handler/retry subgraph since revocation is treated as best-effort.
invariants: Revocation loop never retries a failed domain; failures are only recorded in failed_revocations and the loop continues
revocation_initial_state sets max_retries=0 and renewal_threshold_days=30 (unused placeholder) to satisfy the shared AgentState schema
contracts: build_revocation_graph returns a compiled graph sharing the same AgentState schema as the renewal graph but only populating revocation_* fields

agent-state-shared-schema.name: agent-state-shared-schema
agent-state-shared-schema.description: Single TypedDict (AgentState) shared by both the renewal and revocation graphs, holding config, scan results, in-flight ACME order data, LLM messages, and progress-tracking fields for both lifecycles simultaneously.
invariants: acme_account_key must never be stored in AgentState — only account_key_path is kept, to avoid leaking secrets into LangSmith traces
AcmeOrder list fields (auth_urls, challenge_urls, challenge_tokens, key_authorizations) must remain parallel arrays of equal length
dns_txt_values is empty unless HTTP_CHALLENGE_MODE='dns'
contracts: Any node reading/writing AgentState must only return partial dict updates (LangGraph merges these against schema keys)
messages field is append-only via the add_messages reducer, not replaced wholesale

node-class-registry-factory.name: node-class-registry-factory
node-class-registry-factory.description: Central NODE_REGISTRY dict mapping string node names to node classes, instantiated fresh per get_node() call; used by both graph builders to decouple graph topology definition from node implementation wiring.
invariants: Every registry entry must be a class (type), not an instance or bare function — get_node raises TypeError otherwise
Node names in NODE_REGISTRY must match exactly the names used in build_graph/build_revocation_graph's add_node calls
contracts: get_node(name) always returns a freshly instantiated, callable node exposing __call__(state) -> dict
The same underlying class (e.g. AcmeAccountSetupNode) can be registered under multiple node names (e.g. acme_account_setup and revocation_account_setup)

node-callable-protocol.name: node-callable-protocol
node-callable-protocol.description: Structural typing contract (Protocol) that all graph node classes conform to: a __call__(state) -> dict method, enabling LangGraph to treat class instances as node functions.
invariants: Every node class must implement __call__ that returns a dict of partial state updates, never mutating the input state object directly
contracts: Any object satisfying NodeCallable can be registered as a graph node without inheritance

router-conditional-edge-functions.name: router-conditional-edge-functions
router-conditional-edge-functions.description: Pure routing functions (renewal_router, challenge_router, domain_loop_router, error_action_router) used as LangGraph conditional edge selectors; PickNextDomainNode additionally resets per-domain transient state before starting a new domain.
invariants: error_action_router must never re-parse error_analysis (human prose) for routing decisions — it reads the plain error_action field only
error_action_router defaults to 'skip_domain' if error_action is missing or unrecognized, logging a warning
retry is only honored if retry_count < max_retries, otherwise it degrades to skip_domain even if the handler requested 'retry'
contracts: pick_next_domain always resets current_order, current_nonce, retry_count, and retry_delay_seconds when popping a new domain
domain_loop_router returns 'next_domain' iff pending_renewals is non-empty

ca-detection-advisory-only-for-custom-pr.name: ca-detection-advisory-only-for-custom-provider
ca-detection-advisory-only-for-custom-pr.description: certificate_scanner reads existing certs, computes expiry/renewal-need, and only performs CA-issuer detection (via detect_ca_for_domain) when CA_PROVIDER='custom'; for named providers the configured value is authoritative and detection is skipped entirely.
invariants: detected_ca_provider is always None for named CA providers, regardless of what the actual cert issuer is
letsencrypt and letsencrypt_staging are treated as equivalent when comparing detected vs configured CA to avoid false-positive mismatch warnings
A missing certificate file always sets needs_renewal=True regardless of threshold
contracts: certificate_scanner never raises on a single domain's parse failure — it degrades that domain's record to needs_renewal=True and continues

planner-llm-vs-deterministic-dispatch.name: planner-llm-vs-deterministic-dispatch
planner-llm-vs-deterministic-dispatch.description: renewal_planner branches on config.settings.LLM_DISABLED to either call an LLM for urgent/routine/skip domain classification or use a deterministic priority ordering (no-cert domains first, then soonest-expiring); LLM output is always validated/sanitized against managed_domains before use.
invariants: Every domain in managed_domains must appear in exactly one output bucket (urgent/routine/skip) after validation — missing domains are added to 'routine'
Hallucinated domains not in managed_domains are stripped and logged as warnings, never propagated to pending_renewals
Non-string entries in LLM JSON arrays are dropped rather than crashing the node
Invalid/unparseable JSON from the LLM falls back to renewing all managed domains as 'routine'
Markdown code fences around LLM JSON output are stripped before parsing
contracts: pending_renewals returned by this node contains only validated domain strings from managed_domains, ordered urgent-then-routine

account-key-never-in-state-security-inva.name: account-key-never-in-state-security-invariant
account-key-never-in-state-security-inva.description: acme_account_setup registers or looks up an ACME account, loading/generating the account private key strictly from/to disk (account_key_path) and never placing the key material into AgentState, to avoid leaking secrets into LangGraph/LangSmith traces.
invariants: The account private key object is never included in any node's returned state-update dict
If an account key file exists but the account lookup fails (not found), the node falls through to re-registering a new account rather than erroring
EAB injection (for DigiCert/ZeroSSL/Sectigo) is handled transparently inside the CA client subclass, not in this node
contracts: acme_account_setup always returns acme_account_url and current_nonce on success

order-initializer-multi-domain-san-and-d.name: order-initializer-multi-domain-san-and-dns01-support
order-initializer-multi-domain-san-and-d.description: order_initializer creates an ACME order and fetches all authorizations, building parallel lists (auth_urls, challenge_urls, challenge_tokens, key_authorizations) to support multi-domain SAN orders, and additionally computes DNS TXT values when HTTP_CHALLENGE_MODE='dns'.
invariants: All AcmeOrder list fields (auth_urls, challenge_urls, challenge_tokens, key_authorizations, auth_domains) must stay parallel/equal length across a single order
dns_txt_values is populated only when HTTP_CHALLENGE_MODE='dns'; otherwise it remains an empty list
Missing challenge of the configured type for any authorization aborts order creation with an error_log entry rather than proceeding partially
contracts: order_initializer returns current_order populated with all fields needed by challenge_setup/challenge_verifier for every authorization

challenge-lifecycle-mode-dispatch-and-cl.name: challenge-lifecycle-mode-dispatch-and-cleanup
challenge-lifecycle-mode-dispatch-and-cl.description: ChallengeSetupNode/ChallengeVerifierNode dispatch on HTTP_CHALLENGE_MODE (standalone/webroot/dns) using module-level singleton state (_standalone_server, _dns_provider) to keep challenge infrastructure alive between setup and verify calls, with guaranteed cleanup via _cleanup_challenge after verification (success or failure).
invariants: Standalone mode serves only one token at a time; for multi-domain SAN orders it must stop and restart the server per authorization iteration
_cleanup_challenge must always run after challenge_verifier completes (success or failure) to stop the standalone server, remove webroot files, or delete DNS TXT records

If an authorization is already status='valid' (server-side reuse), challenge_verifier must skip responding to the challenge rather than erroring, since re-responding to a valid authorization is rejected by the CA
Module-level _standalone_server/_dns_provider are not thread-safe/concurrency-safe singletons — only one challenge flow can be in-flight at a time per process
contracts: challenge_verifier returns current_order with status='ready' only if every authorization reached 'valid'
On any authorization failure, current_order.status is set to 'invalid' and cleanup runs before returning

csr-private-key-never-in-state.name: csr-private-key-never-in-state
csr-private-key-never-in-state.description: csr_generator creates a domain key (RSA or ECC per KEY_TYPE) and CSR, writing the private key to disk with 0o600 permissions and storing only the DER-encoded CSR as a hex string in AgentState so it survives LangGraph checkpoint serialization without exposing the key.
invariants: Domain private key material is never placed into AgentState — only csr_der_hex is stored
privkey.pem is always written with mode 0o600 immediately after creation
CSR is DER-encoded (not PEM) since RFC 8555 §7.4 finalize requires DER
contracts: csr_generator writes privkey.pem to the domain's cert directory before returning, so storage_manager can rely on its presence

finalizer-poll-until-certificate-ready.name: finalizer-poll-until-certificate-ready
finalizer-poll-until-certificate-ready.description: OrderFinalizerNode submits the CSR and polls the order until a certificate URL is available; CertDownloaderNode then fetches the replay-safe certificate URL to get the full PEM chain. Both fail gracefully into error_log/current_order.status='invalid' on AcmeError.
invariants: cert_downloader requires certificate_url to already be set in current_order (set only by a prior successful order_finalizer run)
Re-downloading the same certificate_url is safe/idempotent per RFC 8555 §7.4.2
contracts: order_finalizer returns current_order.status='valid' with certificate_url set only on success
cert_downloader returns current_order with full_chain_pem populated only on success

storage-manager-pem-splitting-and-metada.name: storage-manager-pem-splitting-and-metadata
storage-manager-pem-splitting-and-metada.description: storage_manager splits the downloaded full-chain PEM into cert.pem/chain.pem/fullchain.pem, requires privkey.pem to already exist on disk (written earlier by csr_generator), and writes metadata.json capturing issuance details including the CA provider label.
invariants: The first PEM block in full_chain_pem is always treated as the leaf certificate; remaining blocks form the chain
storage_manager fails (adds to failed_renewals, not completed_renewals) if privkey.pem is missing or full_chain_pem is empty — it never partially completes a renewal
contracts: On success, storage_manager appends domain to completed_renewals and records domain->metadata in cert_metadata
On any failure, storage_manager appends domain to failed_renewals and never touches completed_renewals for that domain

error-handler-deterministic-fatal-vs-ret.name: error-handler-deterministic-fatal-vs-retryable-classification
error-handler-deterministic-fatal-vs-ret.description: Deterministic (non-LLM, despite the module comment history) error classifier that checks last error text against fatal ACME error patterns for immediate abort, otherwise applies exponential backoff retry up to max_retries before skipping, always writing error_action as a plain string field for the router.
invariants: Fatal error patterns (unauthorized, accountdoesnotexist, badkey, externalaccountrequired) always trigger 'abort' regardless of retry_count
Backoff delay is min(retry_delay_seconds * 2^(retry_count+1), 300) — capped at 300 seconds
On abort, all pending_renewals are moved into failed_renewals and pending_renewals is cleared — the whole run stops, not just the current domain
error_action is always one of exactly 'retry'|'skip'|'abort', written as a plain field never JSON-encoded
contracts: ErrorHandlerNode always sets error_analysis and error_action in its returned dict
On retry, retry_count is incremented and retry_not_before is set to a future Unix timestamp for retry_scheduler to honor

retry-scheduler-backoff-gate.name: retry-scheduler-backoff-gate
retry-scheduler-backoff-gate.description: Standalone node that blocks (time.sleep) until state['retry_not_before'] elapses before letting the retry loop proceed, decoupling backoff timing from the error decision made in error_handler; includes an unused async variant for future non-blocking execution.
invariants: If retry_not_before is None, the node is a no-op pass-through
retry_not_before is always cleared (set to None) after the wait completes, so a stale timestamp never causes repeated waits
contracts: retry_scheduler always returns before pick_next_domain runs next, guaranteeing backoff delay is honored prior to any retry attempt

deterministic-summary-and-revocation-rep.name: deterministic-summary-and-revocation-reporters
deterministic-summary-and-revocation-rep.description: SummaryReporterNode and RevocationReporterNode compute a deterministic SUCCESS/PARTIAL/FAILED status string from completed/failed/skipped domain lists (or revoked/failed for revocation) and print/log it; no LLM involvement despite prompts.py containing reporter prompt templates.
invariants: status is 'SUCCESS' iff there are zero failures, 'FAILED' iff there are zero completions and at least one failure, else 'PARTIAL'
skipped (renewal) domains are computed as managed_domains minus (completed ∪ failed), never tracked as a separate state list
contracts: Both reporter nodes print the summary to stdout and log it, always returning {'messages': []}

revocation-domain-loop-nodes.name: revocation-domain-loop-nodes
revocation-domain-loop-nodes.description: PickNextRevocationDomainNode and revocation_loop_router mirror the renewal graph's pick_next_domain/domain_loop_router pattern but operate on revocation_targets/current_revocation_domain fields instead, clearing current_nonce between domains.
invariants: current_nonce is always cleared when picking the next revocation domain so cert_revoker fetches a fresh nonce per domain
Calling this node with empty revocation_targets is a caller error (logged as warning) since the router should have already routed to all_done
contracts: revocation_loop_router returns 'next_domain' iff revocation_targets is non-empty

cert-revoker-best-effort-node.name: cert-revoker-best-effort-node
cert-revoker-best-effort-node.description: CertRevokerNode POSTs /revokeCert for the current revocation domain, loading the cert PEM from disk and the account key from disk (never exposing it in state); on any AcmeError it records the failure but always clears current_revocation_domain to allow the loop to continue.
invariants: current_revocation_domain is always cleared (set to None) after a revocation attempt, success or failure, so the loop can proceed
A missing certificate file on disk is treated as a failure, not a crash
revocation reason 0 is passed through unchanged (its omission from the JWS payload is handled downstream in acme/client.py, not here)
contracts: On success, domain is appended to revoked_domains and current_nonce is updated from the response
On AcmeError, domain is appended to failed_revocations and current_nonce falls back to exc.new_nonce or the pre-call nonce

llm-factory-provider-abstraction-with-cl.name: llm-factory-provider-abstraction-with-cli-fallback
llm-factory-provider-abstraction-with-cl.description: make_llm() centralizes LLM chat-model construction across four providers (claude_cli, anthropic, openai, ollama); claude_cli is a langchain-free subprocess shim (ClaudeCLIChatModel) shelling to `claude -p --safe-mode --tools none` to reuse the caller's existing CLI login, while the other three require langchain plus API keys/extras.
invariants: claude_cli path never requires langchain to be installed or ANTHROPIC_API_KEY/OPENAI_API_KEY to be set
For anthropic/openai providers, the corresponding API key must be non-empty or make_llm raises ValueError before attempting construction
ClaudeCLIChatModel always passes --safe-mode and --tools none, ensuring the subprocess call is a pure text completion with no tool/hook/plugin access
If the claude binary is not found on PATH, ClaudeCLIChatModel raises at construction, not at invoke time
contracts: make_llm returns an object exposing .invoke(messages) -> object with a .content string attribute, regardless of provider
ClaudeCLIChatModel.invoke raises RuntimeError if the subprocess exits non-zero or reports is_error in its JSON output

prompts-as-plain-format-strings.name: prompts-as-plain-format-strings
prompts-as-plain-format-strings.description: All LLM prompts (planner, error_handler, summary/revocation reporter) are defined as plain Python string constants using .format() placeholders, kept separate from node logic for easy auditing/versioning of exact wording sent to the LLM.
invariants: PLANNER_SYSTEM/PLANNER_USER instruct strictly JSON-only output with no markdown fences, though planner.py still defensively strips fences since this instruction is not always honored
ERROR_HANDLER_USER always requests one of exactly action='retry'|'skip'|'abort'
contracts: Prompt constants are consumed only by their corresponding node (planner.py, error_handler.py, reporter.py) via .format(**kwargs)
